summary: This cluster focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. Researchers established that the subgenus *Halamphora* is phylogenetically distinct, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and formally described *Amphora terroris* as a new species. A key pattern across the cluster is the reliance on comparative morphological data to validate molecular findings, particularly for distinguishing cryptic lineages within this globally distributed group. The geographic scope encompasses diverse marine and estuarine environments worldwide, reflecting the broad ecological range of *Amphora* species.
